name: Mediterranean Cheese Tart
recipeIngredient: 5 ounces frozen chopped spinach, thawed (5 ozs is 1/2 of 1 package of spinach)
1/3 cup toasted pine nuts or walnuts
1/4 cup oil pack sundried tomatoes, drained
recipeInstructions:
For crust: Generously grease the bottom and sides of a 9-inch springform pan.
Unfold phyllo sheets; cover with plastic wrap or a damp towel to prevent drying.
On a dry surface, place one sheet of phyllo; brush with butter.
Top with another sheet of phyllo, brush with butter, and sprinkle with 1 tablespoon of Parmesan cheese. Repeat with remaining phyllo sheets, butter, and Parmesan.
Using kitchen shears, trim phyllo into an 11-inch circle. Ease phyllo evenly into the prepared pan, pleating as necessary and being careful not to tear the phyllo. Cover pan with damp towel; set aside.
For filling: Cook onions and rosemary in olive oil in a medium saucepan until onions are tender. Stir in spinach and pine nuts (or walnuts). Spread over phyllo-lined springform pan. Set aside.
Lightly beat egg in a medium mixing bowl. Stir in ricotta, feta, tomatoes, and pepper. Carefully spread over spinach mixture. Sprinkle with 1 tablespoon of Parmesan cheese.
Place springform pan on a shallow baking pan on the oven rack. Bake in a 350°F oven for 35 to 40 minutes or until center appears nearly set when shaken. Cool tart in springform pan on a wire rack for 5 minutes. Loosen sides of pan. Cool 15 to 30 minutes more before serving. Makes 12 appetizer servings.
